title: Translating Numbers into Narratives
text:
Translating Numbers into Narratives
Data has no value if stakeholders cannot understand it. The storytelling phase is where I translate raw statistical insights into compelling, publication-ready visualizations that answer direct business questions.
My storytelling philosophy is simple: * Clarity Over Complexity: Eliminate chart junk. Remove unnecessary gridlines and labels so the main insight shines immediately. * Answer the Prompt: Every chart must directly answer a specific business question or challenge a widely held assumption. * Context is Key: Use subtitles, custom sorting, and specific color palettes to guide the viewer’s eye exactly where it needs to go.

title: The Foundation: Data Cleansing
text:
The Foundation: Data Cleansing
In the real world, data is rarely clean. Before any algorithm can be trained or any graph can be plotted, raw data must be wrestled into a usable format. Across my portfolio, data cleansing has been the most critical—and often most time-consuming—step of the analytical process.
My approach to data wrangling involves: * Standardizing Missing Values: Identifying and normalizing inconsistent null placeholders (like -999, blank strings, or "N/A") into standard formats so mathematical functions work correctly. * Format Transformation: Translating highly complex text data (like survey responses) into machine-readable numeric and binary formats using label encoding and one-hot encoding. * Preventing Data Leakage: Rigorously auditing datasets before modeling to remove any columns that might artificially give a machine learning algorithm the “answer key” to the problem it is trying to solve.

title: Uncovering the Shape of the Data
text:
Uncovering the Shape of the Data
Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA) is where the data starts to tell its story. Before applying complex predictive algorithms, I meticulously explore datasets using grouping, aggregations, and initial visualizations to understand underlying distributions and correlations.
Across my portfolio, data exploration has allowed me to: * Identify the Heaviest Hitters: Using feature importance extraction to figure out exactly which variables carry the most mathematical weight. * Engineer New Features: Creating custom metrics—like proportional ratios instead of raw counts—to ensure fair comparisons between different categories. * Validate the Data: Recreating historical charts and filtering data dynamically to ensure the dataset matches known reality.

title: Predicting the Future
text:
Predicting the Future
The final pillar of my portfolio is Machine Learning. This is where historical data is used to train algorithms to recognize complex patterns and predict future outcomes.
In these projects, I demonstrate proficiency in: * Classification Models: Predicting binary or categorical outcomes using Random Forests, Decision Trees, and Gradient Boosting Classifiers. * Regression Models: Predicting continuous, exact numerical values using metrics like Mean Absolute Error (MAE) and R-squared. * Hyperparameter Tuning: Restraining models by limiting tree depth and estimator counts to prevent algorithms from “overfitting” on noisy data.
